jokes.text: I'd have my assistant sit on his naughty stool if he didn't love it so much.
jokes.setup: Jack apologizing for his assistant not announcing Mr. Geiss's arrival
jokes.punchline: Reveals the assistant enjoys punishment, making it ineffective
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy line - corporate speak with sexual undertones, economical delivery

jokes.text: Fire Island, really? I'd love to come out.
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'come out' given Fire Island's gay association
jokes.explanation: Perfect Devon Banks character moment - gay double entendre delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: The head of the Stress Ball division hanged himself.
jokes.setup: Mentioning Bob Dawkin cracked under pressure
jokes.punchline: Ironic death - stress ball executive kills himself from stress
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark irony - the stress relief product executive succumbs to stress, perfectly economical

jokes.text: I want my mother to know this before she dies, so she goes to her grave a defeated woman.
jokes.setup: Jack asking when the promotion will be public
jokes.punchline: Wanting to psychologically defeat his dying mother
jokes.explanation: Peak Jack Donaghy - combines success with maternal psychological warfare, perfectly in character

jokes.text: I guess that rules out the Federal Reserve.
jokes.setup: Geiss saying successor must be someone you trust
jokes.punchline: Implying the Federal Reserve can't be trusted
jokes.explanation: Solid political humor, fits Jack's character but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: You look like Gene Simmons had sex with a basset hound!
jokes.setup: Liz angry at Tracy for computer virus
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific insult combining rock star and dog
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd visual insult, very quotable and unexpected combination

jokes.text: First of all, the subject of the email was 'Check this out!'
jokes.punchline: The most obvious spam subject line presented as irresistible
jokes.explanation: Good character beat for Tracy's impulsiveness, relatable spam humor

jokes.text: I was gonna live with the gorillas.
jokes.setup: Liz lamenting where her life ended up
jokes.punchline: Specific dream of Jane Goodall-style research
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon character detail, sets up later gorilla references

jokes.text: Is it the 8 of diamonds? Nope.
jokes.punchline: Failed magic trick as non-sequitur
jokes.explanation: Minor Kenneth weirdness, establishes scene transition

jokes.text: Pay attention to me! I'm inconsolable.
jokes.punchline: Commanding attention while claiming to be beyond comfort
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy narcissism, contradiction between needing attention and being inconsolable

jokes.text: His flute.
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's simple, earnest correction
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's literalness contrasts with Tracy's over-dramatic vocabulary

jokes.text: If I'm such a bad dad, why are we all dancing?
jokes.setup: Tracy discovering 'Bring your dad to school day'
jokes.punchline: Illogical reasoning that dancing equals good parenting
jokes.explanation: Tracy's flawed logic is perfectly in character, visual implies dance sequence

jokes.text: Everyone knows the only thing we should be ashamed of is our bodies.
jokes.setup: Tracy saying his son is ashamed of him
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's bizarre moral philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth line - twisted innocence and religious shame in one sentence

jokes.text: Like be a senator. Or a wizard.
jokes.setup: Tracy wanting to do something important
jokes.punchline: Equating real political office with fantasy profession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy logic - senator and wizard as equivalent career goals, perfect economy

jokes.text: Eureka! What is it, Tra? We should call Eureka! She always has good ideas.
jokes.setup: Tracy having an idea and shouting 'Eureka!'
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding 'Eureka' as a person's name
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy malapropism, treating exclamation as person's name

jokes.text: For devoting their lives to the jungle and its noble inhabitants.
jokes.setup: Claiming to make fun of gorilla researchers
jokes.punchline: Actually admiring them despite claiming mockery
jokes.explanation: Reveals Liz's true feelings about gorillas, subtle character work

jokes.text: Uh... hugging... so ethnic.
jokes.setup: Liz hugging Jack for his promotion
jokes.punchline: Jack's discomfort with physical affection as 'ethnic'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack WASP discomfort, viewing emotion as foreign concept

jokes.text: Geiss has stacked the Board of Directors with the most reliable collection of sycophantic yesmen this side of an Al Franken book signing.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining the board approval process
jokes.punchline: Comparing corporate yes-men to liberal political event
jokes.explanation: Clever political comparison, though reference dates the joke

jokes.text: His golf cronies, his Army buddies, various unemployable family members, and his hunting dogs.
jokes.punchline: Ending with actual dogs as board members
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity, dogs as literal board members

jokes.text: Why am I smelling self-tanning cream and teeth whitener?
jokes.punchline: Identifying Devon by grooming product smells
jokes.explanation: Perfect Devon Banks characterization through vanity products

jokes.text: Once they cast Clay Aiken in Spamalot, I knew it was only a matter of time for you to show up here.
jokes.punchline: Connecting Broadway casting to Devon's arrival
jokes.explanation: Gay stereotype humor, dates the episode but works for character rivalry

jokes.text: The hottest girls in New York's top boob shacks removing their brassieres.
jokes.punchline: Overly clinical description of strip club
jokes.explanation: Devon's awkward heterosexual overcompensation, 'boob shacks' is memorable

jokes.text: Oh,hey there, little guy.
jokes.punchline: Condescending greeting implying height difference
jokes.explanation: Simple condescension joke, sets up power dynamic

jokes.text: Sounds like soda pop. Damn it! Why is leaving your children a legacy that'll live forever so hard?
jokes.punchline: Frustrated realization his idea is just soda
jokes.explanation: Tracy's grandiose ambitions meeting mundane reality

jokes.text: Yeah,I need to clear my thoughtcicles.
jokes.punchline: Made-up word combining thoughts and popsicles
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy word invention, combines nonsense with meaning

jokes.text: Video games or pornography!
jokes.punchline: Two completely different entertainment forms as equal options
jokes.explanation: Sets up the porn video game idea, typical Tracy bluntness

jokes.text: That's it! Make a porn video game!
jokes.punchline: Combining both pastimes into one impossible project
jokes.explanation: Logical Tracy conclusion, sets up major subplot

jokes.text: So you don't know about him accidentally selling nbc to some germans?
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated scandal about selling network
jokes.explanation: Absurd corporate conspiracy, Germans adds specificity

jokes.text: so take that,liz.
jokes.punchline: Weak victory declaration after being tricked
jokes.explanation: Liz's misplaced confidence after being manipulated

jokes.text: Like taking candy from one of those guys who gives out candy at gay night clubs.
jokes.setup: Devon saying manipulating Liz was easy
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and inappropriate comparison
jokes.explanation: Bizarrely specific Devon reference, inappropriate but memorable

jokes.text: Well,as enticing as that is, I'm straight now.
jokes.punchline: Devon claiming to be straight when convenient
jokes.explanation: Perfect Devon character moment - situational sexuality for advantage

jokes.text: Look,tre, history's greatest perverts have tried: Walt disney,larry flynt, the japanese, but they can't do it because of the uncanny valley.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining why porn video games don't work
jokes.punchline: Listing Disney as a pervert, Japanese as collective pervert
jokes.explanation: Unexpected Disney inclusion, academic tone for pervert discussion

jokes.text: I'm scared! Get me out of there!
jokes.punchline: Tracy's visceral fear reaction to CGI
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy reaction, Tracy's childlike fear

jokes.text: You're like that guy that was always jealous of mozart. Salieri? No,thank you. I already ate.
jokes.punchline: Tracy mishearing as food offer
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy misunderstanding, treats Italian name as food

jokes.text: To get through it, I pretended he was a sandwich.
jokes.setup: Jack praising Liz for handling Devon
jokes.punchline: Bizarre coping mechanism for difficult people
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird Liz coping strategy, highly quotable and memorable

jokes.text: Realistically,teaching improv on cruise ships.
jokes.setup: Jack asking about five year plans
jokes.punchline: Deeply pessimistic but specific career trajectory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz self-awareness, specific enough to be believable

jokes.text: Executives are,like, huh-huh-huh. And I'm,like, haa.
jokes.setup: Liz protesting she's not executive material
jokes.punchline: Different laugh styles to show class distinction
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy through vocal differentiation, class consciousness

jokes.text: Suck it,monkeys! I'm goin' corporate!
jokes.punchline: Abandoning gorilla dreams for money
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and character reversal, money corrupts idealism

jokes.text: let's change the subject to sucking at golf, so donaghy has something to talk about.
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Jack's incompetence at golf
jokes.explanation: Standard golf joke, breaks tension but unremarkable

jokes.text: Well it's business drunk. It's like rich drunk. Either way,it's legal to drive.
jokes.punchline: Jack's twisted logic about drunk driving legality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of drunk types, darkly funny legal logic

jokes.text: You suck,stan!
jokes.punchline: Sudden unprofessional outburst
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe humor, business drunk effects

jokes.text: Button classic? It's hip and homey.
jokes.punchline: Treating button name as marketing genius
jokes.explanation: Corporate buzzword satire, artificial enthusiasm

jokes.text: That's why I love jorgenson here.
jokes.punchline: Casual affection for his fall guy
jokes.explanation: Jack's casual cruelty disguised as affection, sets up later callback

jokes.text: Jeez,jack,offer to buy a girl dinner first. classic lemon!
jokes.setup: Jack saying Liz is ready to meet the old man
jokes.punchline: Liz's sexual interpretation, Jack's pleased reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo humor, Jack's approval of Liz's corporate humor

jokes.text: My genius has come alive, like toys when your back is turned.
jokes.punchline: Comparing genius to Toy Story physics
jokes.explanation: Beautiful Tracy metaphor, childlike wonder applied to adult creativity

jokes.text: Well,I am wearing a cuffed trouser today.
jokes.setup: Tracy seeing erotic potential in Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's innocent acknowledgment of his attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence, treats pants as genuinely attractive

jokes.text: Guess who's got two thumbs, speaks limited french, and hasn't cried once today? This moi.
jokes.punchline: Low bar for daily achievements, mixes English and French
jokes.explanation: Classic Devon overconfidence format, 'not crying' reveals fragility

jokes.text: I know.
jokes.punchline: Pete's defeated acceptance of the money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete moment - knows he can't compete with corporate money

jokes.text: You got the charm and spark of a young leona helmsley.
jokes.setup: Liz's sassy response about working for tips
jokes.punchline: Comparing her to notoriously mean hotel owner
jokes.explanation: Good reference joke, though dates the episode

jokes.text: My god. He's a genius.
jokes.setup: Frank seeing Tracy's porn game work
jokes.punchline: Reluctant admission of Tracy's talent
jokes.explanation: Frank's grudging respect, sets up Tracy's actual achievement

jokes.text: He's like me yesterday. I hate him.
jokes.setup: Liz seeing Devon alone and sad
jokes.punchline: Hating past version of herself in others
jokes.explanation: Perfect psychological insight, self-hatred projected outward

jokes.text: Hoo,boy,got your period?
jokes.setup: Devon trying to be casually masculine
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate question delivered awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Devon's awkward masculinity performance, cringe but character-appropriate

jokes.text: And I love how she enjoys crawling into cabinets and vents and other enclosed spaces. Like behind the dryer, for instance.
jokes.punchline: Listing bizarre behaviors as romantic positives
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd relationship details, implies serious mental issues

jokes.text: I'd marry 1,000 kathy geisses to be ceo.
jokes.punchline: Extreme number showing complete mercenary approach
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Devon's complete cynicism about marriage

jokes.text: It's like when christian won project runway even though rami was clearly the better draper.
jokes.punchline: Comparing corporate succession to fashion reality show
jokes.explanation: Good Jack character beat, unexpected reality TV knowledge

jokes.text: I hate-respect you. And I you.
jokes.punchline: Made-up emotion expressing complex relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect rival relationship summary, 'hate-respect' captures complex dynamic

jokes.text: I hate-respect you. - And I you
jokes.setup: Jack acknowledges his complex feelings toward Banks
jokes.punchline: Banks' reciprocal sentiment using made-up emotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect neologism capturing complex professional rivalry

jokes.text: But he is,in fact, in a diabetic coma, which could've been avoided by what we call 'eating.'
jokes.punchline: Treating basic human need as medical terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Spaceman incompetence, treating obvious solution as medical jargon

jokes.text: But he is, in fact, in a diabetic coma, which could've been avoided by what we call 'eating.'
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman explains Geiss's medical condition
jokes.punchline: Overly clinical explanation for obvious solution
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Spaceman - treating basic human function as medical terminology. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh,damn it. That shot was just a placebo. I was hoping this was psychological.
jokes.punchline: Admitting to giving fake medicine for real emergency
jokes.explanation: Peak Dr. Spaceman incompetence, hoping diabetic coma is psychosomatic

jokes.text: Oh,I'd love to, but we have no way of knowing where the heart is. See,every human is different.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to inject something into heart
jokes.punchline: Doctor not knowing basic anatomy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dr. Spaceman moment, treating universal anatomy as mystery

jokes.text: It was jorgenson's fault!
jokes.setup: Board asking how Geiss got sick
jokes.punchline: Jack immediately blaming his scapegoat
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to scapegoating strategy, Jack's instant blame reflex

jokes.punchline: Not knowing emergency number vs. information
jokes.explanation: Basic incompetence humor, character establishment for Kathy

jokes.text: Because I don't think i can return this 'cause I got business sick all over it when I got home.
jokes.punchline: Vomiting from business drinking, 'business sick'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to business drunk, consequences of corporate lifestyle

jokes.text: I tried to call you, but not on a phone, so you may not have heard me.
jokes.punchline: Admitting to not actually calling
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic, became widely quoted, perfect Devon pettiness

jokes.text: I didn't hear hand washing!
jokes.punchline: Monitoring bathroom sounds from outside
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth germaphobia, inappropriate monitoring of bathroom activities
